patient_brief.presentation: Incidental solid liver lesion on surveillance CT with rising AFP; image-guided percutaneous biopsy requested. A loop of bowel lies along the direct anterior approach to the lesion.

radiology_finding: Solid lesion in the RIGHT hepatic lobe, roughly mid-axial, approximately 2.5 cm deep to the anterior abdominal wall. IMPORTANT: a gas-containing loop of bowel (hollow viscus) is interposed between the anterior abdominal wall and the target along the direct anterior path, so a straight antero-posterior needle to the lesion centroid would traverse bowel. A laterally offset / angled corridor is advised to keep clear of the interposed bowel loop. The major portal venous structures lie posterior and medial to the lesion.
task: Plan a single straight percutaneous needle corridor from the anterior abdominal wall to the center of the lesion for biopsy, avoiding all critical structures (including the interposed bowel loop).

coordinate_frame.note: All coordinates you submit must be world millimetres in this RAS frame.

provided_geometry: NONE — no structure coordinates, no lesion coordinates, no clearances.

thresholds_known.note: Clinical thresholds are provided so you can reason about safety.
